title: Stephanotis faulknerae

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Stephanotis faulknerae include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional medicine, and its extracts are commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a sought-after ingredient in herbal remedies.

This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are primarily native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Stephanotis faulknerae is further classified into the genus Stephanotis, which consists of around 11 species of flowering plants. The genus Stephanotis is a group of evergreen vines that are native to Africa and are known for their fragrant, star-shaped flowers. The classification of Stephanotis faulknerae is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the Apocynaceae family.
